series.title: Rocky Mount Lecture Series
series.subtitle: Tennessee's Founding Story
series.description: Rocky Mount's first-ever lecture series brings scholars and living history interpreters to Northeast Tennessee. Five lectures exploring colonial religion, Revolutionary War heroism, and the birth of Tennessee government.
series.note: All lectures are free with regular admission. Seating is limited.

lectures.title: Colonial Religion on the Frontier

speaker.name: Dr. James P. Byrd
speaker.title: Professor
speaker.bio: Dr. James P. Byrd is a professor at Vanderbilt Divinity School specializing in American religious history. His research focuses on the intersection of religion, politics, and culture in early America.

lectures.description: Explore how faith shaped life on the Tennessee frontier. Dr. Byrd examines the role of religion in colonial America and its influence on early settlers who ventured across the Appalachian Mountains.
topics: The role of churches in frontier communities

lectures.title: Powder for Kings Mountain

speaker.name: Lisa Bennett
speaker.title: Living History Interpreter
speaker.bio: Lisa Bennett brings Mary Patton to life through first-person interpretation. Her portrayal honors the Revolutionary War heroine who supplied gunpowder to the Overmountain Men before their march to Kings Mountain.

lectures.description: Meet Mary Patton, the woman who supplied 500 pounds of gunpowder to the Overmountain Men. Through first-person interpretation, Lisa Bennett brings this unsung Revolutionary War heroine to life.

lectures.title: All In: The Overmountain Men's Gamble

speaker.name: Thomas Bachelor
speaker.title: Historian & Reenactor
speaker.bio: Thomas Bachelor combines academic credentials with authentic reenactor perspective. His research focuses on the military history of the Southern campaigns of the Revolutionary War.

lectures.description: The 1780 march to Kings Mountain was not inevitable victory—it was a desperate gamble. Thomas Bachelor examines the risks taken by Sullivan County men who left their families unprotected to change the course of the Revolution.
lectures.note: Presented one week before Early Frontier Days

lectures.title: William Blount and the Birth of Tennessee Government

speaker.name: Dr. Caroline H. Whitfield
speaker.title: Historian
speaker.bio: Dr. Caroline H. Whitfield specializes in early Tennessee history and the political development of the Southwest Territory.

lectures.description: William Blount, signer of the U.S. Constitution, was appointed by President George Washington to govern the Southwest Territory. Learn how he chose Rocky Mount as his headquarters and laid the foundation for Tennessee statehood.
lectures.note: This is Rocky Mount's story—where Tennessee's government began

lectures.title: The Story of the American Flag

speaker.name: David Doan
speaker.title: Flag Historian
speaker.bio: David Doan is a flag historian who has spent decades researching the evolution of American flags from the colonial era to the present.

lectures.description: From the Grand Union to the Stars and Stripes, explore the evolution of the American flag. Presented as part of America 250 Flag Weekend, this lecture precedes the dedication of Rocky Mount's America 250 commemorative flag.
topics: The meaning of our national symbol

additionalProgramming.title: Cherokee Heritage Weekend

speaker.name: Dr. Daniel Redbird Wolfe
speaker.title: Cherokee Historian
speaker.bio: Dr. Daniel Redbird Wolfe provides Cherokee perspective on frontier Tennessee history.

additionalProgramming.description: While not part of the formal lecture series, this weekend event offers extended programming on Cherokee culture and frontier diplomacy.
additionalProgramming.note: This is a weekend festival, not a single lecture
